highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.196 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds support for organization default models, so admins can now set a preferred model for their whole team via the org console — it appears as 'Org default' in the /model command if you haven't chosen one yourself. Sessions also get readable default names at startup, making it much easier to track and identify past conversations.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.195 Released
highlights.summary: You can now disable mouse click and drag interactions in fullscreen mode using the new CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE_CLICKS environment variable, while still keeping scroll wheel support. An important bug fix also corrects hook matchers so that hyphenated identifiers like 'code-reviewer' or 'mcp__brave-search' now use exact matching instead of accidentally matching substrings.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.191 Released
highlights.summary: The new /rewind command lets you resume a conversation from before you ran /clear, so you no longer lose your context if you accidentally cleared a session. This release also fixes a scroll-jumping bug and a critical issue where stopped background agents would unexpectedly restart.

highlights.title: You Shouldn't Copy-Paste Errors into Claude Code
highlights.summary: This article argues that manually copy-pasting error messages into Claude Code is an inefficient workflow, and there are better ways to pipe errors directly so Claude has full context. By giving Claude the complete error output programmatically, you get more accurate fixes and save time compared to partial pastes.

highlights.title: Show HN: Smart Model Routing Directly in Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor
highlights.summary: Workweave has released an open-source model router that plugs into Claude Code and other AI coding agents to automatically send each request to the most suitable model. This can help reduce costs and improve response quality by matching task complexity to the right model without manual switching.

highlights.title: Claude Code - Full Tutorial for Beginners
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial walks complete beginners through the core features of Claude Code from scratch, making it a great starting point if you've never used it before. It covers setup and basic usage patterns you'll rely on every day.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This compact YouTube tutorial aims to take you from basic familiarity to confident everyday use of Claude Code in just 30 minutes. It's ideal for developers who have already installed Claude Code but want to level up their workflow quickly.

highlights.title: AI Coding Assistant on Mobile: Write Code Anytime, Anywhere (Bilibili)
highlights.summary: This Bilibili video demonstrates how to control an AI coding assistant directly from a smartphone, letting you kick off or review coding tasks even when away from your desk. It's a practical look at mobile-first AI development workflows for developers who want flexibility.

tips_of_the_day: Use /rewind after accidentally running /clear to restore your session context — you no longer have to start over or re-explain your codebase to Claude.

Instead of copy-pasting error messages, pipe your terminal output directly to Claude Code (e.g. 'npm test 2>&1 | claude') so it receives the full, untruncated error with complete stack traces for more accurate fixes.

If your team uses Claude Code, ask your admin to configure an organization default model in the org console — this ensures everyone uses the approved model automatically without each person needing to configure /model themselves.
